content:
A student is analyzing a pure silicon sample at room temperature and observes that its conductivity is relatively low. He explains that this is due to limited free charge carriers.
Which one of the following best explains this behavior?

options.label: B
options.content: Equal number of electrons and holes exist with limited availability

content:
An engineer compares conductors, semiconductors, and insulators based on their energy band gaps.   Which one of the following correctly describes semiconductors?
A) Very large band gap                                    B) No band gap
C) Moderate (small) band gap                          D) Infinite band gap

A student who has a strong understanding of semiconductor concepts is better able to analyze electronic circuits and devices.
Which one of the following best reflects this learning outcome?
A) Avoids practical work                                         B) Improves design and analysis skills
C) Reduces theoretical knowledge                          D) Ignores applications

Semiconductor technology has significantly transformed communication, computing, and automation systems worldwide.
Which one of the following best describes this impact?
A) Increased manual processes                    B) Enabled modern digital and electronic systems
C) Reduced innovation                                 D) Eliminated communication

A student connects a PN junction diode in forward bias and observes a significant current flow after a certain voltage is applied.
Which one of the following best explains this behavior?
A) Increase in depletion region width              B) Reduction in depletion region
C) Increase in resistance                                 D) Absence of carriers

A power supply circuit uses a full-wave rectifier instead of a half-wave rectifier.
Which one of the following is the main advantage of this choice?
A) Lower output voltage                          B) Smoother DC output
C) Higher ripple                                        D) Fewer components

A student increases the base current in a BJT circuit and observes an increase in collector current.  Which one of the following explains this relationship?
A) Current gain property                                 B) Voltage gain only
C) Resistance effect                                         D) Temperature effect

A BJT is selected for an application requiring signal amplification.
Which one of the following is the key reason?
A) Low gain                B) High current gain                   C) No carriers     D) High resistance

A FET is used in a circuit where the input signal controls the output current through voltage variation.   Which one of the following describes this operation?
A) Current-controlled                                       B) Voltage-controlled
C) Temperature-controlled                               D) Resistance-controlled

A MOSFET is used in integrated circuits due to its structure.
Which one of the following is its key feature?
A) PN junction               B) Insulated gate          C) No terminals                D) High current

FET technology contributes to modern devices such as computers and communication systems.  Which one of the following best explains this role?
A) Reduces efficiency                           B) Enables compact and efficient circuits
C) Increases size                                   D) Blocks signals
A transistor has a voltage gain of 100 V/V and a current gain of 1000 A/A. Calculate the power gain in dB.

options.label: A
options.content: 30 Db              B) 40 dB                         C) 50 dB                      D) 60 dB

content: A transistor has a collector resistor RC = 2 kΩ, and the collector current IC = 2 mA. Calculate the collector voltage VCE if VCC = 12 V and RE = 1 kΩ.

options.label: A
options.content: 6 V         B) 8 V                C) 4 V                  D) 2 V

content: Explain the difference between saturation and active regions of a BJT. Which region is used for switching applications?

options.label: A
options.content:
Active for switching, saturation for amplification
B) Saturation for switching, active for amplification
C) Cutoff for amplification, saturation for switching
D) Saturation for both switching and amplification

content: Explain the purpose of coupling capacitors in multistage amplifiers.

options.label: A
options.content:
Block DC, pass AC signals                       B) Pass DC, block AC
C) Increase gain only                                     D) Reduce output impedance

content: A cascade amplifier combines common-emitter and common-base stages. Identify its main advantages.

options.label: A
options.content:
High gain, high input resistance, reduced Miller effect
B) Low gain, low input resistance
C) High input resistance only
D) Low output resistance only

content: A multistage amplifier has midband gain 100 V/V and lower cutoff frequency 50 Hz, upper cutoff 20 kHz. Calculate the bandwidth.

options.label: A
options.content: 19.95 kHz                B) 20 kHz                 C) 50 Hz             D) 19.9 kHz

content: Explain why RC-coupled amplifiers are widely used for audio frequency amplification.

options.label: A
options.content:
Simple design, good midband gain, reasonable bandwidth
B) High gain only
C) Low input impedance only
D) Expensive, complex

content:
Multistage amplifiers are widely used in communication systems.
Which one of the following is the main reason?
A) Low gain
B) High amplification of weak signals
C) Large size
D) High loss

Recording observations from diode, BJT, and FET simulations before labs reflects:
A) Motivation and appreciation of the learning process
B) Random activity
C) Ignorance
D) Low engagement

A) A student keeps trying new EDA simulations independently
B) A student copies classmates’ results
C) A student avoids new tools
D) A student skips labs

options.label: B

options.label: C

options.label: D
options.content: Digital signals cannot be processed by computers

options.label: C

options.label: D
options.content: Traceability to national or international standards

content: In a capacitive sensor, if the distance between the plates (d) is halved, what happens to the capacitance (C)?

To measure the flow of a non-conductive, clean liquid with high accuracy and no pressure drop, which sensor is most appropriate?
An Instrumentation Amplifier is preferred for sensor signal conditioning because it has:

Which sensor type requires an external power source to operate?

A measurement of a constant voltage is taken 10 times. The readings are tightly clustered but are all exactly 0.5V higher than the true value. This instrument is:
Which of the following is an "Absolute" instrument?
Which output presentation element eliminates "Parallax Error"?

options.label: A
options.content: D'Arsonval Meter with a mirrored scale.

options.label: B
options.content: Chart Recorder with a felt-tip pen.

content: A digital multi-meter consistently shows a reading that is 0.05 V higher than the true value every time it is used. How should this error be classified and corrected?

options.label: A
options.content: It is a Gross Error; it is caused by human oversight and cannot be corrected.

options.label: B
options.content: It is a Hysteresis Error; it depends on whether the thickness is increasing or decreasing.

options.label: C
options.content: It is a Systematic Error; it can be corrected by calibrating the instrument or applying an offset.

options.label: D
options.content: It is a Random Error; it can be reduced by taking the average of many readings.

content: A thermocouple (Type K) is connected to a remote PLC. If the 'Cold Junction' at the PLC terminal block rises from 20∘C to 30∘C but the PLC software does not account for this, how will the reported temperature change?

options.label: A
options.content: The reported temperature will increase by 10∘C.

options.label: B
options.content: There will be no change because thermocouples are 'active' sensors.

options.label: C
options.content: The PLC will trigger an 'Open Burn' alarm due to the impedance shift.

options.label: D
options.content: The reported temperature will decrease by approximately 10∘C.

content: Scenario: An Ultrasonic Level Sensor is installed at the top of a sealed tank containing pressurized Nitrogen gas. The liquid level is constant, but as the gas pressure is increased during a filling cycle, the sensor reports that the tank is getting "emptier" (the calculated distance increases). Question: Why is the sensor providing an inaccurate reading?

options.label: A
options.content: The high pressure is physically compressing the ultrasonic transducer.

options.label: B
options.content: The speed of sound increases as gas density/pressure increases, causing the pulse to return faster than the "stored" speed value.

options.label: C
options.content: Nitrogen gas absorbs sound waves, causing the echo to disappear entirely.

options.label: D
options.content: The liquid surface becomes a perfect absorber of sound under high pressure.

content: Scenario: You are installing a temperature control system for a pharmacy store. The cooling system turns ON at 22.0 ∘C and OFF at 21.9 ∘C. You notice the compressor is "chattering" (turning on and off every few seconds), which will eventually destroy the motor. Question: Which change to the Discrete Output Sensor logic will protect the equipment?

options.label: A
options.content: Increase the resolution of the temperature sensor to 0.001∘C.

options.label: B
options.content: Implement Hysteresis (Dead band) so the system turns ON at 23∘C and OFF at 20∘C.

options.label: C
options.content: Replace the digital sensor with an analog thermometer.

options.label: D
options.content: Increase the amplifier gain so the signal reaches the threshold faster

content: A message signal (𝑡) with a bandwidth of B = 30kHz is passed through the system shown in the block diagram below. The carrier frequency is 𝑓𝑐 (where𝑓𝑐 ≫ 𝐵).What is the bandwidth of the resulting output signal 𝒔4(𝑡)?

options.label: A
options.content: It increases with increase in number of quantization levels

options.label: B
options.content: It decreases with increase in number of quantization levels

options.label: C
options.content: It is independent of quantization levels

options.label: D
options.content: It depends only on sampling frequency

content: Vestigial Sideband (VSB) modulation is particularly suitable for:
The primary function of Automatic Gain Control (AGC) in a receiver is to:

options.label: A
options.content: Maintain a constant output signal level despite variations in input signal strength

options.label: B
options.content: Automatically tune the local oscillator frequency

content: Which one of the following is a primary advantage of Angle Modulation (FM/PM) over Amplitude Modulation (AM)?

options.label: C
options.content: Better discrimination against noise and interference

options.label: D
options.content: Lower power consumption at the transmitter

content: A band-limited signal (𝑡) has a maximum frequency component of 𝑊 Hz. According to the Nyquist sampling theorem, what is the minimum sampling rate required to avoid aliasing?
Which of the following receiver performance metrics measures the ability of a communication system to detect weak signals just above the noise floor?
Compare PAM, PWM, and PPM in terms of noise immunity. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.content: PAM has the best noise immunity because amplitude is easy to detect

options.label: B
options.content: PWM and PPM have better noise immunity than PAM because information is in pulse timing, not amplitude

options.label: C
options.content: All three have identical noise immunity

options.label: D
options.content: PPM has the worst noise immunity

content: In a superheterodyne receiver, why is the incoming RF signal combined with the Local Oscillator (LO) signal in the mixer stage?

options.label: A
options.content: To increase the signal bandwidth before it reaches the detector.

options.label: B
options.content: To allow for consistent gain and selectivity at a fixed frequency.

options.label: C
options.content: To eliminate the need for an RF front-end amplifier stage.

options.label: D
options.content: To directly convert the modulated signal into an audio output for the speaker.

content: Compare the bandwidth efficiency and implementation complexity of ASK, FSK, and PSK.Which statement accurately reflects their trade-offs?

options.label: A
options.content: PSK has the best bandwidth efficiency but the most complex demodulation

options.label: B
options.content: ASK has the best bandwidth efficiency and is the most complex

options.label: C
options.content: FSK has the best bandwidth efficiency but is the simplest

options.label: D
options.content: All three have identical bandwidth efficiency

content: Which of the following is an advantage of analog communication systems over digital communication systems?

options.label: B
options.content: Allows integration of voice, video, and data within a single system

options.label: D
options.content: provides better noise immunity and allows signal regeneration

content: In a large interconnected power system, AC transmission is still widely used despite the advantages of HVDC. Which of the following BEST justifies this choice?

options.label: A
options.content: AC systems inherently have lower transmission losses than HVDC

options.label: B
options.content: AC voltage can be easily stepped up or down using transformers, enabling flexible system

options.label: C
options.content: AC systems do not require reactive power compensation

content: In an interconnected power system, economic operation is mainly achieved due to:

options.label: A
options.content: Elimination of all reserve requirements across interconnected areas

options.label: B
options.content: Simultaneous peak demand occurring in all areas

options.label: C
options.content: Sharing of load and generation, reducing the need for installed reserve capacity in each

content: Consider the parameters of an overhead transmission line. Which of the following statements is MOST accurate?

options.label: A
options.content: Line resistance is mainly influenced by conductor spacing and geometry

options.label: B
options.content: Inductance depends only on conductor material and temperature

options.label: C
options.content: Shunt conductance arises due to leakage currents over insulators and atmospheric  conditions

options.label: D
options.content: Capacitance depends only on conductor cross-sectional area

content: A transmission line section passes through a river crossing and is subjected to high mechanical tension and angular deviation. Which insulator type is MOST suitable?
Which of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding transmission line performance?

options.label: A
options.content: In medium transmission lines, shunt capacitance is modeled as lumped elements

options.label: B
options.content: Voltage regulation is affected by load power factor

options.label: C
options.content: Inductive loads operating at leading power factor can cause negative voltage regulation

options.label: D
options.content: Transmission efficiency is mainly affected by I²R losses

content: Corona has both advantages and disadvantages in transmission lines. Which option BEST identifies a beneficial effect of corona?

options.label: B
options.content: Attenuation of high-frequency surges and lightning effects

options.label: C
options.content: Increase in audible noise and radio interference

content: During transmission line design, a sag template is primarily used to:

options.label: B
options.content: Determine optimal tower locations considering clearance, terrain, and conductor sag

content: If the height of transmission towers is increased while keeping conductor spacing constant, what is the MOST likely effect on line parameters?

options.label: A
options.content: Capacitance increases due to higher electric field

options.label: B
options.content: Capacitance decreases due to increased distance from ground

options.label: D
options.content: Resistance increases due to longer conductor length

content: Corona loss in transmission lines is influenced by multiple factors. Which condition will MOST likely increase corona loss?

options.label: A
options.content: Large conductor diameter and low frequency

options.label: B
options.content: Small conductor diameter and high operating voltage

options.label: C
options.content: Low voltage and large conductor spacing

options.label: D
options.content: High conductor diameter and smooth surface

content: Which statement correctly describes power behavior in AC circuits?

options.label: A
options.content: In a purely resistive circuit, current leads voltage by 90°

options.label: B
options.content: In a purely inductive circuit, current leads voltage by 90°

options.label: C
options.content: A capacitor absorbs real power continuously with non-zero average

options.label: D
options.content: In a purely capacitive circuit, current leads voltage by 90°

content: For the same power transfer, increasing transmission voltage by 2 times will reduce line losses approximately by:
A transmission line delivers 100 MW at 0.8 lagging power factor. Improving power factor to unity will:
Which method is MOST effective for improving voltage profile in long transmission lines?
The Ferranti effect becomes significant when:

options.label: C
options.content: Line is long and lightly loaded

content: Which of the following BEST explains skin effect?

options.label: C
options.content: Current concentrates near surface at high frequency

content: A power system engineer wants to reduce transmission losses and improve stability for long-distance bulk power. The BEST choice is:
Load factor is an indicator of:

options.label: B
options.content: Efficiency of utilization of installed capacity

content: Which parameter mainly determines surge impedance loading (SIL)?
Increasing conductor spacing will:
Which factor has the LEAST effect on transmission line resistance?
